infobox.key: [Snowboarding](/wiki/Snowboarding "Snowboarding") at the [2014 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2014_Winter_Olympics "2014 Winter Olympics")
infobox.value: in [Sochi](/wiki/Sochi "Sochi") was held at the [Rosa Khutor Extreme Park](/wiki/Rosa_Khutor_Extreme_Park "Rosa Khutor Extreme Park"). The events were held between 6 and 22 February 2014.[[1]](#cite_note-1) A total of ten snowboarding events were held at Sochi 2014 which include parallel giant slalom, [snowboard cross](/wiki/Snowboard_cross "Snowboard cross"), [half-pipe](/wiki/Half-pipe "Half-pipe"), and the new events of parallel slalom and [slopestyle](/wiki/Slopestyle "Slopestyle").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

**[Snowboarding](/wiki/Snowboarding "Snowboarding") at the [2014 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2014_Winter_Olympics "2014 Winter Olympics")** in [Sochi](/wiki/Sochi "Sochi") was held at the [Rosa Khutor Extreme Park](/wiki/Rosa_Khutor_Extreme_Park "Rosa Khutor Extreme Park"). The events were held between 6 and 22 February 2014.[[1]](#cite_note-1) A total of ten snowboarding events were held at Sochi 2014 which include parallel giant slalom, [snowboard cross](/wiki/Snowboard_cross "Snowboard cross"), [half-pipe](/wiki/Half-pipe "Half-pipe"), and the new events of parallel slalom and [slopestyle](/wiki/Slopestyle "Slopestyle").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

A crash in practice from Norwegian slopestyle snowboarder [Torstein Horgmo](/wiki/Torstein_Horgmo "Torstein Horgmo"), who fractured his collarbone, and complaints from other athletes that some jumps were too steep prompted organisers to modify the slopestyle course in the week before the Games.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Qualification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2014_Winter_Olympics&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Qualification")]

Main article: [Snowboarding at the 2014 Winter Olympics – Qualification](/wiki/Snowboarding_at_the_2014_Winter_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Qualification "Snowboarding at the 2014 Winter Olympics – Qualification")

A total of 252 quota spots were available to athletes to compete at the games. A maximum of 24 athletes could be entered by a [National Olympic Committee](/wiki/National_Olympic_Committee "National Olympic Committee"), with a maximum of 14 men or 14 women. The five different events had different quota numbers allocated to them.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Countries are allowed to enter the same athlete into more than one event, further reducing the number of athletes competing.

## Participating nations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2014_Winter_Olympics&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Participating nations")]

243 athletes from 31 nations participated, with number of athletes in parentheses. [Croatia](/wiki/Croatia_at_the_2014_Winter_Olympics "Croatia at the 2014 Winter Olympics"), [Kazakhstan](/wiki/Kazakhstan_at_the_2014_Winter_Olympics "Kazakhstan at the 2014 Winter Olympics") and [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia_at_the_2014_Winter_Olympics "Serbia at the 2014 Winter Olympics") made their Olympic debuts in the sport.
